name: Retrieve Order History Load Test (Read Operation)
description: Test the /api/v1/orders/history endpoint under concurrent virtual users performing POST requests with date-range filters for 5 minutes to validate average response time, P95 latency, error rate, and throughput requirements for the order history retrieval operation.
corresponding NFR: The /api/v1/orders/history endpoint must maintain an average response time of ≤ 300 ms, a 95th percentile latency of ≤ 600 ms, an error rate of < 0.5%, and a throughput of ≥ 100 requests/sec under concurrent load.
preconditions: The application is deployed and fully accessible in a stable test environment.
The test database is pre-seeded with historical order data for all test user IDs covering the date range 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31, including orders with status completed.
A pool of valid user_id values with existing order history is prepared and parameterised in the test script.
The endpoint is read-only; no authentication is required, or static tokens are used if the environment enforces auth.
No cache invalidation or DB maintenance tasks are scheduled during the test window.
notes: VU count of 60 is a proposed default derived from the throughput target of ≥ 100 req/s with a think time of 1–3 s (Little's Law estimate: 100 req/s × 2 s avg think time ÷ ~3 s avg response ≈ 60 VUs). Ramp-up duration of 60 s and steady-state duration of 300 s are proposed defaults. user_id values must be parameterised from a pre-seeded data pool to ensure realistic filter coverage and avoid repeated single-user queries skewing cache behaviour. If the environment enforces authentication, static tokens with TTL exceeding total test duration (360 s minimum) must be used.
